title: Date Range
description: Date Range is a field for selecting start and end dates with a shared calendar surface, hover preview, and a single combined input value.
intro: Use `ui-date-range` when users need one continuous period instead of two loosely related dates. It fits booking, availability, campaigns, reporting windows, and travel flows where the start and end date belong to the same interaction.

featureSections.title: Basic range selection
featureSections.description: The default pattern is one field that captures a start date and an end date as one coherent value rather than two separate inputs.

featureSections.title: Separator and range preview
featureSections.description: A small API like the separator matters when product language differs, and hover preview makes the end-date choice easier before the range is confirmed.

featureSections.title: Min and max constraints
featureSections.description: Use a constrained window when the selectable period must stay inside a known booking, reporting, or operational range.

featureSections.title: Readonly, disabled, and required states
featureSections.description: State handling is often more important than the range picker chrome itself, so the showcase should demonstrate realistic form states.

featureSections.title: Reactive form integration
featureSections.description: A good showcase should include validation and save flow instead of only an isolated picker shell.

featureSections.title: Booking panel composition
featureSections.description: A practical range picker usually sits inside a booking or filter panel with status context and follow-up actions around the selected period.

accessibility.title: Input and popup behavior
accessibility.body:
`ui-date-range` behaves like one combined text field and opens a calendar dialog-style popup for choosing the start and end dates.

| Element | Behavior |
| --- | --- |
| desktop text input | exposes `aria-haspopup="dialog"` and `aria-expanded` |
| disabled or readonly | prevents opening or changing the range |
| clear action | removes both dates from the current value |

accessibility.title: Keyboard
accessibility.body:
Keyboard handling follows the shared field and popup model.

| Key | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `Tab` / `Shift+Tab` | moves through the field and calendar controls |
| `Enter` / click on trigger actions | opens the range picker panel |
| calendar button activation | selects the start date, then the end date |

accessibility.title: Labels and guidance
accessibility.body: Use a task-specific label such as booking dates, launch period, or stay dates so users understand the period context immediately. Add helper text when the range must stay inside a known window or when the start and end dates have a domain-specific meaning.

apiSections.title: DateRangeComponent - ui-date-range
apiSections.lead: Standalone range field built on the shared field system with a connected calendar overlay. It manages start and end selection as one value object and supports hover preview during selection.

blocks.title: Inputs
rows: Visible separator between the formatted start and end dates.
Controls whether the calendar header can jump through month and year views.

blocks.title: Inherited field inputs
rows: Shared field surface token inherited from `FieldComponent`.

Enables inherited automatic validation error handling from `FieldComponent`.

blocks.title: Outputs and models
rows: Emits the normalized range whenever start or end changes.
Resets both dates in the current range.
Programmatically controls the range picker overlay.
{ startDate: string | null; endDate: string | null }
Stored range object using ISO date strings.

paragraphs: Use this component when the start and end date belong to one interaction. If the dates are unrelated, separate date fields are usually clearer.
The component supports provisional hover preview while the second date is still being chosen, which is especially useful for booking and travel flows.
blocks.title: Notes
